title: Consolidated Data Model
description: Consolidated entity schemas from data model files

job_posting_id.description: Reference to the job posting this application is for

submitted_datetime.description: Full date and time of submission

applicant_id.description: Anonymous or pseudonymous applicant identifier (for privacy)

timeline.description: Timeline events for this application (nested children)

review_id.description: Reference to review created from this application experience

application.description:
Structured record of a job application submission. Tracks when applications
were submitted and links to timeline events (responses, interviews, outcomes).
This is the core data point for tracking hiring process transparency.

application_id.description: Reference to the application this event belongs to

event_datetime.description: Full date and time of the event

response_time_days.description: Days from application submission to this event (computed from event_date - application.submitted_date)

was_promise_kept.description: Whether a timeline promise was kept (if applicable)

notes.description: Additional notes about the event (structured, not free-text review)

application_timeline.description:
Structured events in the application lifecycle: responses, interviews,
ghosting, offers, rejections. These are verifiable events that form
the basis of trust metrics. Examples: "Applied Jan 15", "First response
Jan 18", "Ghosted after second interview", "Offer received Feb 1".

company_id.description: Unique company identifier (e.g., domain name, organization ID)

job_postings.description: Job postings for this company (nested children)

company.description:
Generic company or organization entity that can be used across different
market types. This is a core entity that provides basic company information
without market-specific relationships.

job_posting_id.description: Unique job posting identifier (e.g., job board ID, internal ID)

company_id.description: Reference to the company posting this job

applications.description: Applications submitted for this job posting (nested children)

promises.description: Promises made in this job posting (nested children)

personal_vouches.description: Personal vouches from people who believe this is a real opportunity (nested children)

job_posting.description:
Individual job postings with advertised details including salary ranges,
requirements, and timeline promises. These postings are linked to
applications and reviews.

vouch_id.description: Unique vouch identifier (matches core vouch.vouch_id if linked)

job_posting_id.description: Reference to the job posting being vouched for (subject_id in core vouch)

core_vouch_id.description: Optional reference to core vouch entity for reputation weighting and revocation

source_identity.description: GitHub UID or Public Key of the voucher (from core vouch concept)

claim_type.description: What specifically is being vouched for (from core vouch: EXISTENCE, INTEGRITY, TERMS)

evidence_link.description: Reference to a commit, file, or external proof that supports the claim (from core vouch)

source_seniority.description: Computed age of the source account in days (e.g., GitHub account age). Used for reputation weighting.

source_activity_index.description: Historical 'Proof of Work' metric (e.g., commits/contributions count). Used for reputation weighting.

vouch_weight.description: The final 'force' this vouch applies to the Market Gravity. Computed from source reputation metrics.

valid_from.description: Date from which the vouch is valid (YYYY-MM-DD). Ensures vouches don't stay 'green' if market conditions change.

valid_until.description: Date until which the vouch is valid (YYYY-MM-DD). Optional expiration date for time-based validity.

revocation_digest.description: If the voucher discovers the 'Originator' lied, they can issue a 'Revocation' which is appended to the ledger, immediately flipping the go_nogo_signal. This is a cryptographic digest/hash of the revocation.

revoked_date.description: Date when the vouch was revoked (YYYY-MM-DD), if applicable

voucher_relationship.description: Job market specific: Relationship of the voucher to the company/position

confidence_level.description: Job market specific: Confidence level (1-5) of the voucher in the legitimacy of the posting

vouch_statement.description: Optional statement from the voucher explaining why they believe this is a real opportunity

is_verified.description: Whether the vouch has been verified (e.g., through cross-referencing or evidence)

verification_notes.description: Notes about verification of the vouch

personal_vouch.description:
Extends the core vouch entity with job market specific fields. A personal vouch
for a job posting represents a conditional transfer of reputation where someone
goes on record stating they believe a job posting represents a real opportunity.
This implements the Trust Chain concept from the core vouch model, with job-market
specific relationship context.

job_posting_id.description: Reference to job posting where promise was made

promise_text.description: Text of the promise (e.g., 'We'll respond within 2 weeks')

promised_value_min.description: Minimum promised value (for numeric promises like salary)

promised_value_max.description: Maximum promised value (for numeric promises like salary)

promised_timeline_days.description: Promised timeline in days (e.g., 14 for '2 weeks')

was_kept.description: Whether the promise was kept (verified against actual outcomes)

verification_date.description: Date when promise fulfillment was verified (YYYY-MM-DD)

promise.description:
Tracks promises made by employers: salary ranges, response timelines,
process steps, etc. These are compared against actual outcomes to measure
transparency. Examples: "We'll respond within 2 weeks", "Salary range
$80k-$100k", "3 interview rounds".

company_id.description: Reference to the company being reviewed

application_id.description: Reference to the application this review is based on

transparency_score.description: Transparency score (1-5): Were promises kept? Were timelines accurate?

respect_score.description: Experiential respect score (1-5): Was humanity honored? Was communication respectful?

efficiency_score.description: Time efficiency score (1-5): Was effort proportional to outcome?

overall_score.description: Overall score (average of transparency, respect, and efficiency scores, computed at application layer)

was_ghosted.description: Whether the applicant was ghosted (no response after engagement)

offer_pct_below.description: Percentage below advertised minimum (computed from offer_salary vs advertised range, if applicable)

go_nogo_signal.description: Simple go/no-go signal for quick decision making

verification.description: Verification data for this review (nested children)

review.description:
Structured, verifiable reviews of hiring experiences. These are not free-text
reviews but structured data points that can be cross-verified. Based on
three dimensions: Transparency (were promises kept?), Experiential Respect
(was humanity honored?), and Time Efficiency (was effort proportional to outcome?).
These reviews feed into all interface views: Trust Stock Market, Google Maps,
Wikipedia, and Simple Signals.

review_id.description: Reference to the review being verified

is_consistent.description: Whether review is consistent with other reports

verifiability_weight.description: Weight assigned based on verifiability (0.0 to 1.0)

manipulation_risk_score.description: Risk score for manipulation (0.0 = low risk, 1.0 = high risk)

cross_reference_count.description: Number of other reviews that cross-reference this one

verification.description:
Cross-verification data, manipulation detection, and evidence weighting
for reviews. This implements the Verification & Anti-Gamification Layer
that ensures data reliability. Includes cross-referencing for consistency,
weighting by verifiability, and detection of coordinated manipulation.

subject_id.description: Unique identifier of the thing being vouched for (the 'Opportunity' or subject)

source_identity.description: GitHub UID or Public Key of the voucher (the source making the vouch)

claim_type.description: What specifically is being vouched for (EXISTENCE: thing exists, INTEGRITY: thing is legitimate, TERMS: terms are accurate)

evidence_link.description: Reference to a commit, file, or external proof that supports the claim

source_seniority.description: Computed age of the source account in days (e.g., GitHub account age). Used for reputation weighting.

source_activity_index.description: Historical 'Proof of Work' metric (e.g., commits/contributions count). Used for reputation weighting.

vouch_weight.description: The final 'force' this vouch applies to the Market Gravity. Computed from source reputation metrics.

valid_from.description: Date from which the vouch is valid (YYYY-MM-DD). Ensures vouches don't stay 'green' if market conditions change.

valid_until.description: Date until which the vouch is valid (YYYY-MM-DD). Optional expiration date for time-based validity.

revocation_digest.description: If the voucher discovers the 'Originator' lied, they can issue a 'Revocation' which is appended to the ledger, immediately flipping the go_nogo_signal. This is a cryptographic digest/hash of the revocation.

revoked_date.description: Date when the vouch was revoked (YYYY-MM-DD), if applicable

vouch_statement.description: Optional statement from the voucher explaining the vouch

vouch.description:
A market-agnostic vouch entity that represents a conditional transfer of
reputation. A vouch is a structural link between a Subject (the opportunity
or thing being vouched for) and a Source (the identity making the vouch).
This implements the Trust Chain concept where vouches can be weighted by
source reputation, have time-based validity, and support revocation.

change.description: Change log entry describing a versioned modification to the consolidated data model or data.

git_commit_hash.description: Git commit hash corresponding to this change
